title: URL Slug Pins
description: Pinned expected URL slugs for every Streamlit page in `app/pages/`. Per APP-URL1 (owner: Ace, per META-CF), Streamlit Cloud derives each page's URL slug from its filename by stripping the leading `N_` numeric prefix and converting underscores. If Streamlit changes its slugification rules (case, separator, prefix stripping) the slug for every page silently shifts and every downstream consumer — breadcrumb components, cross-page `st.page_link(...)` calls, external deep links cited in `acceptance.md` and stakeholder feedback — breaks without a visible error. Pinning the expected slug and asserting it at acceptance-time turns a silent class of regression into a blocking gate. Registry-based breadcrumb rendering reads `expected_slug` here instead of inferring from the filename.

x-version.description: Semver of the pins instance (separate from this schema's x-version). Bumped per META-CF whenever a page is added, renamed, or moved; any `expected_slug` change is a breaking major bump because external links depend on the slug.

x-owner.description: Single agent accountable for the pins instance. Must be 'ace'.

x-generated.description: ISO-8601 date the instance was last regenerated.

streamlit_version_observed.description: Streamlit version under which these slugs were observed (e.g., '1.39.0'). Consumer smoke test flags a warning if `streamlit.__version__` at acceptance time differs from this value.

notes.description: Freeform notes (e.g., observed slug rule: strip leading `N_`, lowercase, keep underscores as underscores).

pages.description: Map of page_file (relative path under app/pages/) -> slug pin entry.

expected_slug.description: The exact slug Streamlit is expected to derive from this filename at page-registry build time. Observed rule for Streamlit 1.39.x: strip leading `N_` numeric prefix + underscore, keep remaining underscores as underscores, do not modify case. Example: `9_hy_ig_v2_spy_story.py` -> `hy_ig_v2_spy_story`.

pair_id.description: Pair the page belongs to (e.g., 'hy_ig_v2_spy'). Landing / cross-pair pages use 'landing'.

canonical_breadcrumb.description: Optional human-readable breadcrumb label rendered by the breadcrumb component (replaces inferred title-casing). Example: 'HY-IG v2 -> Story'.

inbound_links.description: Cross-page links in other app/pages/*.py that target this page via st.page_link. Used by the slug-pin smoke test to assert every link still resolves after a Streamlit upgrade.
